Oklahoma · State Licensing Reference

Dietitian
Licensed Dietitian (LD) Guide

Issued by the Oklahoma Licensed Dietitians Committee under the Oklahoma State Board of Medical Licensure and Supervision. Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$120
$120 initial application fee (background check fees extra)
Renewal
$100
Annual (by Oct 31st)
Processing Time
4 to 6 Weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
1 Year
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Oklahoma Licensed Dietitians Committee

Mailing Address

P.O. Box 18256
Oklahoma City, OK 73154

Physical Address

101 NE 51st Street
Oklahoma City, OK 73105

Phone & Fax

Phone: (405) 962-1400

Fax: (405) 962-1499

Email

licensing@okmedicalboard.org

Credential Categories

Oklahoma Dietitian License Types

Licensed Dietitian (LD)

The credential required to practice dietetics in the state of Oklahoma. Supervised by the Licensed Dietitians Committee under the Medical Board. Note: Oklahoma does not license the title 'Nutritionist'.

Provisional Licensed Dietitian (PLD)

Available for applicants who have completed the academic and supervised practice requirements but have not yet passed the CDR examination. Must practice under the supervision of a Licensed Dietitian. Valid for one year.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Oklahoma Licensed Dietitian (LD)

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete between 1,000 and 1,200 hours of board-approved supervised practice or an accredited dietetic internship.

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) national examination.

Submit the Online Application

Apply through the Oklahoma State Board of Medical Licensure online portal and pay the $120 application fee.

Complete Background Check & Provide Documents

All applicants must submit to an Extended Background Check. You must also submit primary source verification of your education and passing CDR exam score.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • CDR Registration VerificationProof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration sent directly to the Board.
  • Official College TranscriptsSent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.
  • Extended Background CheckProcessed through the Oklahoma State Bureau of Investigation (OSBI).
  • Evidence of Legal StatusForm documenting your citizenship or qualified alien status.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

Oklahoma offers a Provisional Licensed Dietitian (PLD) distinct credential for applicants who have met the education and supervised practice requirements and are preparing for the CDR exam. A Provisional Licensed Dietitian must practice under the direct supervision of an Oklahoma Licensed Dietitian. The provisional license is valid for one year.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Applicants currently licensed in another state may apply via endorsement if the other state's licensing requirements are substantially equivalent to or stricter than Oklahoma's. The standard application, fee, and verification of all out-of-state licenses are required.

Frequently Asked Questions

Oklahoma Dietitian FAQ

How much does an Oklahoma dietitian license cost?

The initial license application fee is $120. Additional fees apply for the required background check.

When does the Oklahoma license need to be renewed?

The Oklahoma licensed dietitian credential expires annually on October 31st. The renewal fee is $100.

Does Oklahoma license Nutritionists?

No, Oklahoma only issues licenses for Dietitians (Licensed Dietitian). There are no specific licensing requirements or title protections for individuals claiming to be 'nutritionists' in the state.
